div1.entryFree:
Uncertainty, 1) want of reliability; doubtfulness, precariousness (of things); inconsistency, inconstancy (of persons): our fortunes . . . which now we hold at much u. H4A I, 3, 299. and here remain with your u. Cor. III, 3, 124.
2) something not certainly and exactly known: until I know this sure u. Err. II, 2, 187.
